list_page_content.Achievements:
''For tips and strategies on completing achievements, see [[achievement tip]]s.''

'''Achievements''' are unlocked after completing [[Challenge]]s, reaching certain [[completion mark|milestones]], achieving hidden goals, or unlocking certain [[item]]s. In the PC version, every Steam achievement exactly corresponds to one secret in-game, whereas only a handful of secrets are represented by PSN trophies in the PlayStation version of the game.

If Isaac is unable to unlock achievements, a small icon of a crossed-out trophy {{no unlocks}} will be displayed below the {{e|Key}}'s counter. Conditions which can disable the ability to unlock achievements include:
* Using a pre-determined [[seed]]. {{dlc|a}} Certain cosmetic and difficulty-increasing [[seed|special seeds]] will still allow unlocks.
* During a [[Challenge]], although the achievement for completing the challenge is unaffected.
* {{dlc|a}} During a [[Daily Challenge]], although the achievements associated with this game mode are unaffected.
* {{dlc|a+}} Playing with any enabled [[mod]] (unless {{e|Mom}} has been beaten).
* {{dlc|a+}} During [[Victory Lap]]s.
* {{dlc|r}} If the [[Debug Console]] is enabled (unless {{e|Mom}} has been beaten).
* {{dlc|r+}} Joining an in progress [[Online (open beta)|Online]] run.

Rebirth has a total of 178 unlockable secrets. {{dlc|a}} [[Afterbirth]] added 98 new secrets, for a total of 276. {{dlc|a+}} [[Afterbirth †]] added 63 new secrets, for a total of 339. {{dlc|a+}} [[Booster Pack #5]] added 64 new secrets, for a total of 403. {{dlc|r}} [[Repentance]] added 234 new secrets, for a total of 637.
{{dlc|r+}} [[Repentance+]] added 4 new secrets, for a total of 641.

Inside the stats menu, while having the "secrets" menu open, pressing Alt + F2 will sync unlocks with Steam achievements. If you have Steam achievements for secrets that haven't been unlocked in-game, they will be unlocked by doing this.
NOTE: using this on a second file will take your first file and copy it (because Steam achievements don't consider the file you have unlocked it in).

== Notes ==
* {{dlc+|r}} The {{a|5 Nights at Mom's}} achievement is bugged in various ways:
** {{dlc|nr+}} {{plat|PC}} Because the game uses 32-bit architecture and has [[Custom Challenges#Characters (playertype)|41]] characters, the game counts wins twice for the first 9 characters. That means if in your streak you play once with a character from Isaac to Lazarus, you will get the achievement with 4 wins. If you play with two characters from Isaac to Lazarus, you will get the achievement with 3 wins. Since the game uses 64-bit architecture on consoles, this bug is PC-only.
** Using normal [[Characters]] to get a win and then using its tainted counterpart won't work, only one of them will work for obtaining the achievement.
*** Additionally, due to integer overflow, some tainted characters share the same bitmask with some normal characters. The game resets the achievement streak counter if two runs are done with characters with the same bitmask. {{c|Tainted Lilith}} has the same bitmask as {{c|Isaac}}. Here are the pairs of characters that have the same bitmask : Tainted Keeper and Magdalene, Tainted Apollyon and Cain, Tainted Forgotten and Lazarus, Tainted Bethany and ???, Tainted Jacob and Eve, Tainted Dead Lazarus and Samson, Tainted Jacob in Lost form and Azazel. If you did a run with Isaac or Lilith, do not finish a run with Tainted Lilith or the counter will reset. Not confirmed yet but by the same logic as the bug above, this one is likely PC-only too.
** The achievement does not unlock in Co-op Repentance, even if the five wins were done with different characters for each player.
** Modded characters do not interfere with the streaks, and do not reset nor increment the counter.{{dlc-}}

* {{dlc+|a+}} Despite unlocking {{T|Locust of War}}, achievement 310 is named "Locust of Wrath".{{dlc-}}

list_page_content.Challenges: '''Challenges''' are unique runs in the game. Isaac typically starts each challenge with one or more specific items which makes normal play more difficult. With few exceptions, {{R|Treasure Room}}s are not available during challenges. Challenges are all set in {{mode|normal}} normal mode except for [[I RULE!]]. Each challenge unlocks new content for the game, such as [[rune]]s, when completed. Players, with the advent of modding, can create their own [[Custom Challenges]] too. == General Strategy == Most challenges are designed to increase the difficulty of the game in a unique fashion. This doesn't mean all challenges are especially difficult. Challenges like [[Computer Savvy]] can be quite easy to beat, other challenges compensate for this by being especially punishing; [[Solar System]] being a good example of this. Each challenge has certain strategies that can be employed to make the run easier. However, there are a few strategies that can be applied to most challenges. * Challenges can be played in [[co-op]], allowing a second player to contribute to the run without being hindered in the same way Isaac is. This is especially useful in any challenge where Isaac is [[Blindfolded]], as the second player will be allowed to fire tears normally. ** {{dlc|r}} If other players join the game in the first room for true co-op, they will not get to select a character and will be forced to play as the character the challenge has you play as, ex. for [[Blue Bomber]], all players will be forced to play as {{c|???}} no matter what. (This excludes untrue co-op because of co-op babies.) * Due to the lack of {{R|Treasure Room}}s, {{R|Devil Room}}s become much more valuable. Some challenges can be made much easier by making just one deal, however many challenges reduce Isaac's health to the extent that Devil Deals become too risky. ** Some rooms (including {{R|Crawl Space}}s) may have Treasure Room items by default. Since these items are random, results can vary from run to run. *** {{dlc+|r}} Makes {{i|We Need To Go Deeper!}} useful, since it can open {{R|Crawl Space}}s each floor.{{dlc-}} ** Because locked Treasure Rooms don't spawn, keys can be used far more liberally. Also, [[trinket]]s and [[item]]s that generate more coins, along with upgraded {{R|Shop}}s, can help Isaac acquire a greater range of powerful items. * {{i|The Bible}} instantly kills {{E|Mom}} and {{E|Mom's Heart}} / {{E|It Lives!}}. All challenges require defeating Mom, and most require defeating Mom's Heart or It Lives!, making this an extremely powerful item, especially in challenges where Mom or Mom's Heart / It Lives! is the final boss. ** {{i|Pandora's Box}} will spawn {{I|The Bible}} if used in {{S|Womb}}/{{S|Utero}}/{{S|Scarred Womb}} II, making it a reliable way to acquire The Bible. ** {{p|XV - The Devil?}} can also do {{i|The Bible}}'s job. * {{R|Curse Room}}s can spawn items, making them valuable. * {{dlc|na}} In some challenges, {{R|Dice Room}}s and the {{i|D4}} are very useful, allowing that challenge's starting items to be rerolled, thus removing most of the difficulty. NOTE: This changes depending on the challenge. Most challenge pages contain details on the results of rerolling. ** {{t|Butter!}} can also remove starter items. * [[Blindfolded]] challenges are arguably the most difficult challenges due to Isaac's inability to fire tears. There are several methods to work around this. ** Familiars can provide an alternative method of dealing damage. Powerful familiars like {{i|Lil Brimstone}}, {{i|Succubus}}, {{i|Incubus}}, and {{i|Twisted Pair}} are therefore highly useful items. ** {{i|The Boomerang}}, {{i|The Candle}}, and {{i|Red Candle}} are also useful for blindfolded challenges, as they recharge based on a timer, rather than an amount of cleared rooms. ** {{e|Golden Bomb}} also provides a powerful damage output method for a floor. ** {{i|Clicker|dlc=nr}} can allow Isaac to change into {{C|Lilith}}, whose {{i|Incubus}} overrides the blindfolded effect by default. ** Some active items like {{i|Tammy's Head}} can be really helpful, providing an alternative method of dealing damage. ** {{dlc+|r}} {{i|Maw Of The Void}}, {{i|Revelation}}, and {{i|Montezuma's Revenge}} can be charged and fired even while Blindfolded, giving you very powerful attacks when you're not supposed to be able to shoot.{{dlc-}} ** Most useful items while blindfolded gain no benefit from the Tears, Range, or Shot Speed stats. As a result, items like {{i|False PHD}} can be used to make gains in other stats at effectively no cost. * {{i|Chaos}} is a powerful item in challenges, as it can allow powerful items to be found more commonly, such as in the Shop or after defeating bosses. * Defeating a boss beyond the specified end of the challenge (e.g. defeating {{E|Mom's Heart}} when the goal is to defeat {{E|Mom}}) will still spawn the trophy. ** {{dlc|anr}} In some longer challenges (e.g. [[XXXXXXXXL]]), using a {{R|Sacrifice Room}} until it teleports Isaac to the {{S|Dark Room}} is a viable strategy. Once in the Dark Room, the trophy can be spawned by defeating {{E|The Lamb}}. *** {{dlc+|a+nr}} In a similar vein, all runs can be completed by killing {{e|Delirium}}, meaning that the player can beat a challenge through an alternative method such as defeating {{E|Hush}} then traveling to {{S|The Void}}; however, this is generally extremely difficult given the restrictions challenges place on the player.{{dlc-}} == Notes == * Apart from the achievement related to beating the challenge, all achievements are locked. Also, no {{M|Donation Machine}}s will appear in any challenge. * {{C|Isaac}} does not start any challenge with {{i|The D6}}. * {{dlc+|r}} Items with tag [[Item Tags nochallenge|nochallenge]] are excluded during challenges. ** {{i|Lazarus' Rags}}, {{i|Judas' Shadow}}, {{i|Ankh}}, and {{i|Clicker}} are excluded, preventing Isaac from changing to other characters. *** {{dlc|nr+}} {{t|Broken Ankh}} is still available. *** {{dlc|r+}} {{t|Broken Ankh}}, {{t|Missing Poster}} and {{t|Mysterious Paper}} are also excluded ** {{i|R Key}} is also excluded. ** Excluded items are considered as if they have not been unlocked, making it impossible to get these items by {{i|Spindown Dice}}, {{i|Bag of Crafting}}, or {{i|Death Certificate}}. ** It is, however, possible for [[Aprils Fool]]'s effect on active items to cause {{I|Clicker}} to activate.{{dlc-}} == Challenges List == {{challenge table}} {{nav|challenge|fr=Défis|ru=Испытания}} [[Category:Challenges| ]]
list_page_content.Items:
<!-- {{#ev:twitchvod|v312958626|400|right}} -->
'''Items''' are an integral part of the gameplay of ''The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth''. They modify Isaac's [[attribute]]s, grant or modify [[tear effect]]s, and much more.

Items are classified into several distinct types and groups. Some items are passive, which add an effect permanently to Isaac, while others only activate when used.

* The original ''The Binding of Isaac'' has 196 items.
* ''Rebirth'' has a total of 341 items.
* {{dlc|a}} ''[[Afterbirth]]'' added 95 new items, for a total of 436 items.
* {{dlc|a+}} ''[[Afterbirth †]]'' added 111 new items, for a total of 547 items.
* {{dlc|r}} ''[[Repentance]]'' added 169 new items, for a total of 716 items. An update for Repentance, [[V1.7.5]], added 3 new items, for a total of 719 items.

In ''[[Afterbirth †]]'' and prior, 13 items are considered [[Special Item|special]] and are less likely to appear if Isaac has already come across other special items. The ''[[Repentance]]'' expansion removed the Special Item mechanic, replacing it with the [[Item Pool#Item Weight|item weight]] and [[item quality]] mechanics.

For a list of which items can potentially appear in a given [[special room]] or under certain conditions, see [[item pool]] and [[Item Tags]].

For the grids of items that appear in the stats menu, see [[Collection Page]].

Not to be confused with [[cards and runes]], [[pills]], and [[trinkets]].

__TOC__

== Activated Collectibles ==
Unless Isaac has {{i|Schoolbag}}, only one activated item can be held at a time, with the old collectible being placed on a pedestal if a new activated item is picked up. Most activated items need to recharge before use, with the recharge bar filling either by clearing rooms or recharging over time. Other activated items do not have a charge bar, and, with a few exceptions (such as {{i|How to Jump}}), will instead either disappear after a single use or require resources such as [[coins]] or [[health]]. Some activated items will also grant passive effects as long as they are equipped.

{{dlc|r}} In some special circumstances, activated items can be placed in the consumable slot instead of the standard activated item slot. This counts as both an extra activated item slot and an extra consumable slot, meaning other activated items and consumables can be picked up as normal. Items in this slot are used with the consumable key, and can't be lost through any means (unless they're consumed like items from {{t|Dice Bag}}). Otherwise, they function the same as other activated items.

=== Notes ===
* [[Battery Bum]]s and most [[Batteries]] charge item(s) in the activated item slot first, then the item in the consumable slot. The exception to this is the {{e|Mega Battery}}, which recharges the items in both slots.
* Most trinkets that work with activated items (e.g. {{t|Vibrant Bulb}}, {{t|Panic Button}}, etc.) ignore items in the consumable slot.
* There are a total of 171 activated items.

{{collectible table | type = activated }}

== Passive Collectibles ==
Passive collectibles are vast and diverse, varying wildly in effect and strength. Some simply improve Isaac's stats, add [[health]], or provide a [[familiar]]. Some completely change the way the run can play.

* Other common effects include:
** Isaac's tears gaining [[Tear effects]] or causing [[Status Effects]] on hitting enemies.
** Isaac's tears being replaced with a completely different weapon, such as a [[Mom's Knife|knife]].
** Isaac's bombs gaining new effects, often with a bonus supply of extra bombs.
** Triggering a special effect when Isaac takes damage, or kills an enemy.
** Creating additional [[pickups]], either when first collected, or over time.
** Improving active item cooldowns.
** Granting permanent [[flight]].

An unlimited amount of passive collectibles can be held at a time, and the effects they grant usually last until the end of that run.

* Removing a passive item is only possible in a few rare ways, and usually comes with severe drawbacks, such as {{I|D4}} and {{I|Genesis}} rerolling ''all'' passive items, or {{I|Clicker}} removing the last passive item collected at the cost of changing the active character.

* {{dlc|r}}{{C|Tainted Isaac}} is limited to 8 passive items (12 with {{I|Birthright}}) at a time, and can freely swap out old passive items once his inventory is full.

Nearly all passive collectibles alter Isaac's appearance when collected, with newer items replacing the cosmetic effects of older items.

{{collectible table | type = passive }}

== Trivia ==
* {{t|Wiggle Worm}} was an item in the original ''The Binding of Isaac''; however, in ''Rebirth'', it was changed into a [[trinket]].
** {{i|The Polaroid}} was a trinket in the original ''The Binding of Isaac'', but was changed into an item. Interestingly, these changes keep the count of items and trinkets from the original game the same.
* There are a total of 170 active items, with 74 in ''Rebirth'', 19 in ''Afterbirth'', 30 in ''Afterbirth+'', and 47 in ''Repentance''. Of those, 138 items possess a charge bar, with 61 items in ''Rebirth'', 16 in ''Afterbirth'', 24 in ''Afterbirth+'', and 37 in ''Repentance''.
* There are a total of 547 passive items, with 267 in ''Rebirth'', 76 in ''Afterbirth'', 81 in ''Afterbirth+'', and 123 in ''Repentance''.
** From ''Rebirth'' to ''Afterbirth+'', there has been about a 1:4 ratio of active items to passive items.
* {{dlc|a+}} ''Afterbirth+'s'' original item count was 89. The [[Booster Pack]]s added 42 items.

list_page_content.Trinkets:
{{#ev:youtube|e_vlRV6lFx4|280|right}}
'''Trinkets''' are a type of collectible with their designated slot that can provide passive bonuses. Only one trinket can be carried at a time and must be dropped if you wish to carry another. Some of these limitations can be bypassed with certain [[item]]s; {{i|Mom's Purse}} and {{i|Belly Button}} allow two trinkets to be held at once, {{dlc|a+|}} and trinkets can be absorbed by {{i|Smelter}}, {{i|Marbles}}, or [[Gulp!]]. Absorbed trinkets do not take up a trinket slot and act as permanent passive effects. Compared to items with a similar effect, trinket effects are typically more subtle, less powerful, or less reliable. For example, the {{t|Callus}} only prevents damage from spikes and creep, while many items that do the same also grant Isaac [[flight]]. The original Binding of Isaac DLC 'The Wrath of the Lamb' introduced Trinkets as a mechanic and had 33 trinkets. Rebirth has a total of 60 trinkets. [[Afterbirth]] added 29 new trinkets, for a total of 89 trinkets. [[Afterbirth †]] added 38 new trinkets, for a total of 127 trinkets. [[The Binding of Isaac: Repentance|Repentance]] added 61 new trinkets, for a total of 188 trinkets. {{dlc|r}} All trinkets have a very rare 2% chance to spawn as [[Golden Trinket]]s. * Unlocked by: {{achievement unlock|Golden Trinket}} == Notes == * It is possible to drop a trinket by holding down the key or button bound to the "Drop" function for three seconds. (Doing this is not possible with the {{t|Tick}}.) * While held, {{i|Mom's Box}} doubles the effect of most trinkets. ** {{dlc|r}} A Golden Trinket with Mom's Box will have a tripled effect. * Trinkets appear in a set order based on the run's seed, and each trinket can usually only be encountered once. ** Trinkets which are set drops, such as {{t|Mysterious Paper}} from {{i|Divorce Papers}} and {{t|The Left Hand}} from {{e|Ultra Pride}}, can appear even if encountered previously. ** Other set drops, such as {{t|Swallowed Penny}} from a [[urn]] or {{t|Umbilical Cord}} from a [[polyp]], will not appear if already seen. ** If enough trinkets are spawned, e.g. through Mom's Box, the cycle loops and begins spawning trinkets that were already encountered. * {{dlc|nr}} If two of the same trinkets are gathered, Isaac can have both by using [[Gulp!]] on one and then picking up the other. This rarely has any useful purpose. * {{dlc+|r}} Multiples of the same trinkets sometimes stack as expected, and two copies of the same trinket can be held simultaneously with {{i|Belly Button}} or {{i|Mom's Purse}}. However, a large number of trinkets don't stack, especially the ones that are more complicated than a simple stat up.{{dlc-}} * Trinkets cannot be duplicated with items such as {{i|Diplopia}} or {{P|Jera}}. * {{dlc+|a+nr}} Activating the {{i|D1}} on a trinket will duplicate the trinket.{{dlc-}} * {{dlc+|r}} Activating the {{i|D1}} on a trinket will spawn a new, different trinket.{{dlc-}} == All Trinkets == {{trinket table}} == Trivia == * {{i|The Polaroid}} was originally a trinket in the original Binding of Isaac, but it was changed into an [[item]]. It had the trinket ID 47 which is now empty. ** {{t|Wiggle Worm}} was an item in the original Binding of Isaac; however, in Rebirth, it was changed into a [[trinket]]. Interestingly, these changes keep the count of items and trinkets from the original game the same. * {{dlc|a+}} Afterbirth+'s original trinket count was 29. The [[Booster Pack]]s added 9 trinkets. * [[enboi:Game Squid|Game Squid]] is the only trinket from the original Binding of Isaac that wasn't implemented into Rebirth or any of its DLCs. This is likely due to it having been added in the Eternal Edition update after Rebirth's release. ** {{T|Chewed Pen}}, a trinket added in Repentance, has a nearly identical effect to Game Squid. * The game has a set "order" of trinkets that will appear. So, for example, if a trinket is encountered as a room drop, then going back in time with the {{i|Glowing Hourglass}}, the next trinket that drops from any source will always be that same trinket. This is also why {{i|Guppy's Eye}} can show the same trinket in two different chests; as the next drop is technically that trinket, no matter which chest is opened. Opening that chest and getting that trinket will cause the other chest to change its drop to a different trinket. {{nav|fr=Babioles}} [[Category:Objects]]
list_page_content.Rooms:
Each [[floor]] of the game is made up of a number of '''Rooms'''. The number of Rooms on each floor generally increases as the game progresses. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Normal Rooms == Normal Rooms make up the majority of the Rooms on a floor. These Rooms typically contain enemies and may drop a [[pickup]] or chest after all enemies are killed. Many Rooms also contain various [[pickup]]s, [[chest]]s, [[item]]s, and [[obstacle]]s. Isaac cannot leave a Room with enemies until they are all killed or until a door is opened with an explosion. If Isaac is currently holding an [[active item]], completing a Room with enemies will add one charge. Rooms can come in various sizes; while normal-sized Rooms are the most common, double and quadruple-sized Rooms can also appear. {{dlc|a}} L-shaped triple-sized Rooms, narrow hallways, and tiny Rooms can also appear. Additionally, completing a quadruple-sized Room or an L-shaped Room will add two charges to Isaac's active item. * {{dlc|nr+}} The last wave of {{r|Boss Rush}} adds only one charge. Sometimes rooms may be "flooded" with water, blood, or lava, depending on the [[Chapters|Chapter]]. Flooded rooms allow for unique interactions with some [[Items]], such as {{i|Jacob's Ladder}}, and enemies. Normal rooms have occurrence weight assigned to them. Some rooms occur very rarely. Usually, those rooms contain numerous or rare pickups or chests — sometimes even items. === {{dlc|a}} [[Closet]]s === Closets are rare, small, dead end Normal Rooms. They often contain a unique arrangement of items, objects, or pickups. == Special Rooms == === {{r|Boss Room}}s === [[file:Boss Room door.png|98px]] Boss Rooms contain one of the many [[Boss]]es, with victory granting Isaac an item from the {{ip|Boss|text=Boss pool}}, [[heart]]s (depending on difficulty), and a trap door leading to the next level. They typically spawn in the room that's as far from the starting room as possible. {{dlc|r+}}Boss Rooms emit continuous roaring noises, which dynamically pan left or right depending on Isaac's position relative to the door. The roaring stops when the boss is defeated. ==== {{r|Mini-Boss Room}} ==== Mini-Boss Rooms are identical in appearance to normal Rooms and contain one of the [[Seven Deadly Sins]]. * Mini-Boss Rooms are barred shut upon entering and are unable to be escaped or opened, even by explosions, until the Mini-Bosses have been defeated. * Mini-Boss Rooms have a map icon but don't have a special door. ** The map icon is only revealed once the room is entered, or if Isaac has an item that reveals map icons like {{i|The Compass}}. === [[Treasure Room]]s === ==== Normal/Golden {{r|Treasure Room}}s ==== {{slideshow | [[file:itemroomdoor.png|98px]] | [[file:lockeditemroomdoor.png|98px]] }} A Treasure Room, or Item Room, is guaranteed to appear on all floors from {{s|Basement}} 1 to {{s|Depths}} 2. From Basement 2 and on, this Room requires a [[key]] to enter. The Room contains one item from the {{ip|Treasure Room|text=Treasure Room item pool}}. Various [[obstacle]]s may be present, and occasionally {{E|Fly|a|display=Flies}} or {{E|Greed Gaper|a+nr}}s/{{E|Bulb|r}}s will be present. The Room has a small chance to contain two items from the treasure pool that can both be collected, and a ''very'' small chance of containing no item pedestal at all. {{dlc+|r}} On {{s|Downpour}} / {{s|Dross}}, {{s|Mines}} / {{s|Ashpit}}, and {{s|Mausoleum}} / {{s|Gehenna}}, Treasure Rooms always contain at least two items, with one of them being hidden, similar to {{Cu|Blind}}.{{dlc-}} {{dlc|a}} Golden Treasure Rooms always require one key to open in [[Greed Mode]] and Greedier Mode. {{dlc|a+}} Golden Treasure Rooms can generate in the {{s|Womb}} / {{s|Utero}} if the {{t|Bloody Crown}} trinket is equipped when the floor is generated. {{dlc|r}} Golden Treasure Rooms can generate in {{s|Sheol}} with the {{t|Wicked Crown}} trinket equipped, and can generate in the {{s|Cathedral}} with the {{t|Holy Crown}} trinket equipped. ==== {{dlc|a}} Silver Treasure Rooms ==== [[File:Silver_Treasure_Room_opened.png|98px]] Silver Treasure Rooms are Rooms found in [[Greed Mode]] and Greedier Mode. They always contain one item from the {{ip|Greed Boss|text=Greed "Boss" Item Pool}}, and require no keys to open, being open from the start of the floor. ==== {{r|Devil Treasure Room}}s ==== {{slideshow | [[File:Devil Treasure Room door locked.png|98px]] | [[File:Devil Treasure Room door.png|98px]] }} When carrying the {{t|Devil's Crown}} trinket, any newly found or not walked into Treasure Room becomes a Red Treasure Room, which contain items from the Devil Room pool for the price of heart containers. The prices are the same as normally required in regular [[Devil Room|Devil Deals]]. === {{r|Planetarium}}s === {{slideshow | [[file:Planetarium door locked.png|98px]] | [[file:Planetarium door.png|98px]] }} Planetariums are similar to Treasure Rooms, but only contain one powerful celestial item belonging to the {{ip|Planetarium|text=Planetarium item pool}}, items exclusive to Planetarium Rooms. Planetariums have an additive and higher chance of spawning if the player avoids walking into Treasure Rooms. === {{r|Shop}}s === {{slideshow |{{Dlc|r}}[[file:Shop door locked.png|98px]] |{{Dlc|r}}[[file:Shop door.png|98px]] }} {{dlc|nr}}[[file:Locked Door.png|98px]] Shops require a key to open. They sell various items and pickups in exchange for coins. Shops start at level 0 and only sell 2 things, but Shops at higher levels sell more things. They can be upgraded through the {{M|Donation Machine}}, which can be commonly found in the Shops and sometimes in an [[Arcade]].
Shops also have a chance of being replaced by a miniboss Room containing {{E|Greed}} or {{E|Super Greed}}. {{dlc|a+}} Shops can generate in the {{s|Womb}}/{{s|Utero}} if the {{t|Silver Dollar}} trinket is equipped when the floor is generated. {{dlc|r}} The Shop spawned on the first floor is free to enter. {{dlc|r}} Shops can generate in {{s|Sheol}} with the {{t|Wicked Crown}} trinket equipped, as well as {{s|Cathedral}} with the {{t|Holy Crown}} trinket equipped. === {{r|Arcade}}s === [[file:Arcade Door.png|98px]] These Rooms are locked and require one coin to be opened. Arcades can only spawn on even floors (Basement II, Caves II, Depths II, Womb II, etc) if the previous floor was completed with 5 or more coins. [[Arcade]]s can contain [[machine]]s, [[beggar]]s, and other objects. {{dlc+|r}} Arcades spawn on the first floor of the alternate chapters ({{s|Downpour}} I, {{s|Mines}} I, {{s|Mausoleum}} I, {{s|Corpse}} I).{{dlc-}} === [[Challenge Room]]s === ==== Normal {{r|Challenge Room}} ==== [[file:Challenge Room door.png|98px]] Challenge Rooms can only spawn if, upon entering the floor, Isaac's total health (red, black, and soul hearts) is equal to or greater than his number of heart containers. Same conditions are required to be able to enter it. Upon picking up its contents, Isaac has to fight three waves of enemies. ==== {{r|Boss Challenge Room}} ==== [[file:Boss Challenge Room Door.png|98px]] Boss Challenge Rooms generate with the same conditions as normal challenge rooms upon entering the second floor of a [[Chapter]]. Their contents are similar to the Normal Challenge Room, but upon picking up the contents, Isaac faces two waves of [[Boss]]es instead of normal monsters. {{dlc|nr}} They can only be entered if Isaac has one or less red heart. {{dlc|r}} They can only be entered if Isaac's total health (red, black, and soul hearts) is equal to or less than 1 full heart, and always contain an item from the {{ip|Boss|text=Boss Item Pool}}. === {{r|Curse Room}}s === [[file:Curse Room Door.png|98px]] Curse Rooms are special rooms with spikes on the door that hurt Isaac when entering and exiting the Room. Curse Rooms can contain various things, ranging from enemies, red chests, to even pedestal [[item]]s from the {{ip|Curse Room|text=Curse Room Item Pool}}. Isaac doesn't take damage upon entering the room if he has [[flight]] (still takes damage upon exiting). === {{r|Sacrifice Room}}s === {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Sacrifice Room door.png|98px]] Sacrifice Rooms are special rooms with a chance to spawn a {{E|Chest}} or other preset rewards after Isaac damages himself on the spikes featured at the center of the Room. Sacrifice Rooms have a higher chance to spawn if Isaac's total health (red, black, and soul hearts) is equal to or greater than his number of heart containers. {{dlc|a}} Sacrifice Rooms can now pay out with a much wider variety of rewards. {{dlc|r}} Has a unique bloody door, replacing its old, generic one. === {{r|Secret Room}}s === [[file:Secret Room Door.png|98px]] Secret Rooms must be bombed into, and are usually surrounded by 3 or 4 Rooms. Secret Rooms usually contain {{e|Shopkeeper}}s and various [[pickup]]s. === {{r|Super Secret Room}}s === [[file:Secret Room Door.png|98px]] Super Secret Rooms must be bombed into, and are only located next to one other non-special Room. They tend to spawn closer to the Boss Room. Super Secret Rooms can contain a variety of [[pickup]]s. === {{r|Ultra Secret Room}}s === [[file:Secret Room Door.png|98px]] Ultra Secret Rooms are unique secret rooms, found when venturing one Red Room outside the normal map using the {{I|Red Key|x}}, {{T|Crystal Key|x}}, {{P|Cracked Key|x}}, {{P|Soul of Cain|x}}, or by teleporting to it with {{P|XVIII - The Moon?|x}}. These map-expanding items allow you to create Red Rooms, and if correctly found, one of those new Red Rooms will have a Secret Room passageway to the Ultra Secret Room already opened. The Ultra Secret Room itself contains {{e|Key}}s, [[Blocks|key blocks]], and/or {{e|Shopkeeper}}s, and one item from the {{ip|Ultra Secret Room|text=Ultra Secret Room item pool}} for its pedestal rewards. === {{r|Libraries}} === {{slideshow | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Library door locked.png|98px]] | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Library door.png|98px]] }} {{dlc|nr}}[[file:Locked Door.png|98px]] Libraries require a key to be entered and can contain {{ip|Library|text=book items}}, [[card]]s, [[rune]]s, and [[trinket]]s. === {{r|Devil Room}}s === [[file:Devil Room door.png|98px]] These Rooms allow the player to make [[Devil Deal|deals with the Devil]] by trading [[health]] for {{ip|Devil Room|text=items}}. Devil Rooms can also contain {{e|Red Chest}}s, {{e|Black Heart}}s, [[spikes]], or [[monster]]s along with its signature devil statue. They may also have {{e|Fire Place}}s or {{E|Red Fire Place}}s which have a chance to drop [[coins]], {{e|Red Heart}}s, and more when extinguished. [[Devil Room]]s have a chance to contain {{E|Krampus}} rather than any items, if Krampus is unlocked. === {{r|Angel Room}}s === [[file:Angel Room door.png|98px]] Angel Rooms contain an angel statue and one or more {{ip|Angel Room|text=items}} to choose from, {{e|Soul Heart}}s, or {{e|Eternal Heart}}s. The Angel Room has a chance to replace the Devil Room if certain conditions are met. They may also have {{E|Blue Fire Place}}s or {{E|Purple Fire Place}}s, which have a chance to drop {{e|Soul Heart}}s when extinguished. {{dlc+|r}} Can now have a {{M|Confessional}} in them.{{dlc-}} === {{r|Vault}}s === {{slideshow | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Vault door locked.png|98px]] | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Vault door.png|98px]] }} {{dlc|nr}}[[file:Double Locked Room.png|98px]] Vaults require two keys to open and can contain various things, ranging from [[item]]s to {{M|Donation Machine}}s. === {{r|Dice Room}}s === {{slideshow | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Dice Room door locked.png|98px]] | {{Dlc|r}}[[file:Dice Room door.png|98px]] }} {{dlc|nr}}[[file:Double Locked Room.png|98px]] Dice Rooms require two keys to open. Stepping onto the center of the die activates an [[dice room#effects|effect]], determined by the number of pips imprinted on the face of the large red die embedded in the floor, . * Dice Rooms, when spawning, replace a sacrifice room that also could have spawned on the floor. They have a higher chance of spawning over a sacrifice room spawn if you have 2 or more keys. * {{dlc|a+}} Dice Rooms now have a small image in the corner of the large red die that also tells the effect of stepping on it. === {{r|Clean Bedroom}}s and {{r|Dirty Bedroom}}s === [[file:Double Boarded Room.png|98px]] Bedrooms require two bombs to open. Bedrooms contain a bed that can be used once to restore all of the player's health, or if they have no red hearts, they give you 3 soul hearts {{dlc|na}}and add one charge to their active item. Depending on if the Bedroom is Clean or Dirty (the main difference being the state of the bed and wall color), bombing the center of the carpet will reveal a [[crawl space]] (Clean) or a trapdoor (Dirty) leading to the next floor. === {{r|Crawl Space}}s === [[file:Secret_Trap_Door.png|98px]] These Rooms are entered through Trapdoors with ladders in them. They can rarely be found under destroyed rocks. They can also be found using {{i|We Need To Go Deeper!}}, {{p|Ehwaz}}, {{p|XXI - The World?}} or under a {{r|Clean Bedroom}}'s rug. These Rooms are viewed from the side, rather than the top like the rest of the Rooms in the game. Crawl Spaces usually contain an item or various [[pickup]]s. Rarely, the Crawl Space can lead to a {{r|Black Market}}. ==== {{r|Black Market}}s ==== Black markets are accessed through Rare Crawl Spaces. These Rooms are 2 Rooms wide and have {{ip|Shop|text=shop items}} that can all be bought for 1 heart container, like [[Devil Room|devil deals]]. Black Markets also have a trapdoor to the next floor and various [[machine]]s or {{m|Beggar}}s. === {{dlc+|r}} [[The Stairway|Angel Room Shops]] === Angel Room Shops are special holy shops, accessed if Isaac has {{I|The Stairway}}. The Stairway spawns a ladder in the starting room of each floor leading to each floor's Angel Room shop, a [[Shop]] that sells {{ip|Angel Room|text=Angel deal items}}, has angel-related pickups, sometimes includes a coin [[beggar]] or a {{M|Confessional}}, and always includes an angel statue. This ladder disappears upon leaving the room.{{dlc-}} === {{dlc|r}} [[Member Card|Secret Shops]] === [[file:Secret_Shop_Trapdoor.png|98px]] Secret shops are accessed by buying a {{I|Member Card|x}}, opening up a 'special' Shop Trapdoor that leads to the floor's underground shop. Secret shops have a wider variety of items and pickups, sold at various expensive prices. === {{r|Red Room}}s === {{slideshow | [[file:Red Room Door outline.png|98px]] | [[file:Red Room Door opened.png|98px]] }} Red Rooms are special rooms created when using the {{I|Red Key|x}} (and Red Key wisps spawned with {{I|Book of Virtues}}), {{T|Crystal Key|x}}, {{P|Cracked Key|x}}, or the {{P|Soul of Cain|x}}. The Red Rooms can’t be opened without these items, and are spawned into existence at the closest red outline door when the item is used. The new Red Room can be a normal Room on this floor, or any special Room, aside from extra Ultra Secret Rooms and Boss Rooms. Red Rooms can't spawn where there aren't 4 connections out of the Room, not spawning if a touching room contains a bottomless pit on one wall, already is a secret room, or is a thin closet/hallway room. Red rooms can only be spawned in an 13x13 radius from the the center room of the map, or six rooms from the center room in any direction; going any farther will teleport Isaac into the I AM ERROR Room. === {{r|I AM ERROR}} Rooms === I AM ERROR Rooms have a small chance of being reached upon using [[Telepills]], and can be reached with other items such as {{I|Undefined}} and {{I|Red Key}}. Every I AM ERROR Room contains a trapdoor to the next floor along with various [[pickup]]s or [[item]]s. The only way out of an I AM ERROR Room is through the trapdoor or by teleporting out. Using {{I|Teleport 2.0|x}} when all other rooms have been explored guarantees to be teleported in an I AM ERROR room. The I AM ERROR Room is a running gag within games that is a reference to the "I AM ERROR" easter egg in Zelda II: The Adventure of Link (1987) === {{r|Boss Rush}} === [[file:Boss Rush Door.png|98px]] If the player manages to beat {{e|Mom}} in under 20 minutes from the start of a run (visually indicated by the clock icon that appears during the cutscene between floors), a crack will open in one of the walls of the Boss Room, allowing entry to a large Room with 2, 4 or 8 item pedestals in the center or the corners, often blocked by [[rock]]s or [[spikes]]. Taking one of the presented items will destroy the others and begin the Boss Rush. * {{I|Mama Mega!}} or {{i|Mega Bean|r+}} can be used to open the Boss Rush entrance in the Mom Boss Room even after the 20 minute deadline, allowing access to the Boss Rush Room. * {{dlc+|a+}} The {{i|Broken Shovel 1}} opens the Boss Rush Room, even after the 20 minute deadline. The Trapdoor to the next Floor is also closed until Boss Rush is completed.{{dlc-}} * {{dlc|r}} Mausoleum Mom also spawns the Boss Rush Room if defeated within 25 minutes. === [[Mega Satan|Mega Satan Arena]] === [[file:Mega_Satan_Door.png|98px]] This long Room is only found in the starting Room of {{s|Chest}} and {{s|Dark Room}}. Opening this door requires an item such as {{i|Dad's Key}} or the two key pieces from [[Angel]]s. Once the Room is entered, Isaac will have to face off against {{E|Mega Satan}}. === {{dlc|a}} [[??? (Floor)|??? Entrance]] === [[file:Blue Womb Door.png|98px]] If the player manages to beat {{e|Mom's Heart}} or {{E|It Lives!}} in under 30 minutes (visually indicated by the clock icon that appears during the cutscene between floors), a crack will open in the wall of the Boss Room upon defeating it, containing a room with a Trapdoor to {{s|???}}. === {{dlc|r}} [[Mother|Mother Arena]] === [[file:Mother Trapdoor.png|98px]] Mother's arena is accessible by a large pit in the ground in the Boss Room of {{s|Corpse}} II. Dropping into the pit leads to Mother's unique transforming boss room. === {{dlc|a}} Greed Exit Rooms === [[file:Greed_Exit_Door_opened.png|98px]] Exit Rooms are Rooms with the defining feature of having a Trapdoor/exit to the next Floor. Greed Exit Rooms start barred off and unbombable, like miniboss rooms, but become accessible once {{mode|greed}} ten / {{mode|greedier}} eleven waves of enemies are defeated. * Greed Exit Rooms have a special door, having a special black exit doorway in the main greed room, but having a normal floor colored doorframe when in the room itself. Exit Rooms may contain pickups, chests, and enemies. === {{dlc+|r}} Alternate Path Exit Rooms === {{slideshow | [[file:Downpour entrance door.png|98px]] | [[file:Downpour entrance door locked.png|98px]] }}{{slideshow | [[file:Mines entrance door.png|98px]] | [[file:Mines entrance door locked 2.png|98px]] | [[file:Mines entrance door locked 1.png|98px]] }}{{slideshow | [[file:Mausoleum entrance door.png|98px]] | [[file:Mausoleum entrance door locked.png|98px]] }} When unlocked, these Rooms appear attached to a Main Path Boss Room once the [[Boss]] is defeated (or appear at the end of Alternate Path [[Chapter]]). These Rooms contain an entrance to the Alternate Path Floors. These Rooms have different requirements to open them, based on the [[Chapter]] they are barring entrance to: # In floors 1-2, the door requires a key to unlock the entrance to {{s|Downpour}} or {{s|Dross}}. # In floors 3-4, the door requires two bombs to unlock the entrance to {{s|Mines}} or {{s|Ashpit}}. # In floors 5-6, the door requires sacrificing two hearts to unlock the entrance to {{s|Mausoleum}} or {{s|Gehenna}} (except on {{s|Depths}} II and its variants, where this door doesn't appear).{{dlc-}} ==== {{a|A Strange Door}} Exit Room ==== [[File:A_Strange_Door_locked.png|98px]] When unlocked, this room appears above the starting room of the second floor of Chapter 3, containing a trapdoor leading to a special version of the second floor of Chapter 3.5. This door is generally opened by obtaining and sacrificing either {{i|The Polaroid}} or the {{i|The Negative}}. The door can also be opened with {{t|Broken Padlock}}, {{i|Cracked Orb}}, {{t|Faded Polaroid}}, {{p|Soul of Cain}}, or {{i|Sharp Key}}. === {{dlc|a+}} [[Grave Room]]s === Grave Rooms are Rooms that appear in the {{s|Dark Room}} which only contain 1-3 [[Dirt Patch]]es. {{c|The Forgotten}} is unlocked by reaching this room and digging with {{i|Mom's Shovel}}. {{slideshow | [[file:Dirt Patch.png]] | [[file:Dirt Patch Forgotten.png]] }} === {{dlc+|r}} {{r|Mirror Room}} === [[File:Mirror_Door.png|98px]] The Mirror Room is a single special room found on the second floor of Chapter 1.5, containing a single mirror on either the left or right doorway location. This mirror can be broken by explosions, but also can be entered to explore the [[Mirrored World]], a replica of the current floor, to find {{i|Knife Piece 1}}. The mirror can only be entered by {{C|The Lost}}, {{C|Tainted Lost}}, {{C|Tainted Forgotten}}, and {{C|Tainted Jacob}}'s ghost form. All other characters must touch the {{e|White Fire Place}} which always generates on the second floor of Chapter 1.5 in order to temporarily become The Lost in order to enter the mirror.{{dlc-}} === {{dlc+|r}} {{r|Mine Cart Room}} === {{slideshow | [[file:Mineshaft_Door_locked.png|98px]] | [[file:Mineshaft_Door_opened.png|98px]] | [[file:Mineshaft_Door_blocked.png|98px]] }} The Mine Cart Room is a single special room found on the second floor of Chapter 2.5 only if Isaac already has {{i|Knife Piece 1}}. It contains a Yellow Button, a single minecart, a blocked door at the top of the room and occasionally some enemies. There are 2 other {{e|Yellow Button}}s located randomly on the same floor and pressing all 3 repairs the minecart's track allowing access through the blocked door. Alternatively having [[Flight]] and 2 bombs also allows access through the door. The area past the blocked door contains {{i| Knife Piece 2}} and a chase sequence with {{E|Mother's Shadow}}. The door is permanently blocked after leaving the sub-area.{{dlc-}} == Notes == * In regular Rooms with unreachable enemies, the doors will automatically open after a while to prevent Isaac from becoming unable to progress. See [[User:Blcd/RandomTidbits#Room Anti-softlock|here]] for the specific details. ** This will not occur if Isaac has [[flight]] or if there is a boss or miniboss in the Room. ** This will not occur in Rooms with {{E|Pressure Plate}}s that do not have enemies or only have indestructible enemies such as {{E|Wall Hugger}}s or {{E|Stone Grimace}}s. * Rooms that have obstacles in the middle covered by pits can often drop the room reward in the place of the destroyed obstacle. Because of that, it is advised avoiding touching that obstacle if you really need pickups. == Gallery == File:Binding Of Octorock.jpg|[https://twitter.com/edmundmcmillen/status/1235474907489689601 The original concept art] for "Octorock," an early model of Isaac, and gameplay features. Interestingly enough, XL Rooms and L-shaped Room layouts were shown but never implemented until Rebirth and its first DLC, Afterbirth, respectively. File:Room Concepts.jpg|Afterbirth Room layout concepts on the upper left side by Edmund McMillen. {{nav|room|fr=Salles}}
list_page_content.Transformations:
{{header transformations}} '''Transformations''' alter Isaac's appearance and grant him special abilities. A transformation occurs when obtaining at least three different [[item]]s from a particular set of items.
These collectibles always share a particular theme that corresponds with the transformation. == Transformation Sets == {{transformation table}} == Requirements == * Items only need to have been acquired once in order to count. ** {{dlc|nr}} Duplicate items (like those acquired from {{i|Diplopia}}) do not count toward transformations when picked up. Only the first instance of an item Isaac owns will count. ** {{dlc|r}} Duplicate items are all counted. ** {{dlc|r+}} Duplicate items are not counted for the Necromancer transformation. ** [[Activated collectible]]s (e.g. {{i|Guppy's Head}}) can be picked up once and then swapped away with another without any loss to transformation progress. * {{dlc|nr}} Rerolling items with a {{r|Dice Room}}, the {{i|D4}}, or the {{i|D100}} will not remove progress towards the transformation. ** {{dlc|r}} The transformation will be lost if the items required are rerolled or otherwise lost, eg. {{I|Sacrificial Altar|dlc=x}}. * {{dlc+|a}} Familiars gained from {{i|Cambion Conception}} or {{i|Immaculate Conception}} do not count towards transformation progress.{{dlc-}} * [[Trinket]]s do not count toward progress for any transformation, with the exceptions of {{t|Kid's Drawing}} that counts for Guppy transformation, {{t|Bob's Bladder|r+}} for Bob, and {{t|A Missing Page|r+}} for Necromancer. * [[Pill]]s only count toward [[Adult]] and [[Stompy]], they do not count toward progress for any other transformation. * Characters that start with an item that counts towards a transformation do count as one of the three items needed for a transformation, such as Judas starting with [[The Book of Belial]]. * {{dlc+|r}} Only the first character to pick up an item will gain progress towards its transformation. This applies in [[Repentance Co-Op|co-op]], or when playing as {{c|Jacob and Esau}} or {{c|Tainted Lazarus}}.{{dlc-}} * {{C|Tainted Isaac}} will not lose a transformation nor progress towards it if he replaces an item that counts towards that transformation. == Notes == * Transformations can be combined, keeping the effects of all transformations with no effects overriding others. * Transforming into [[Beelzebub]] for the first time will unlock the ''Lord of the Flies'' [[achievement]] and {{i|Angry Fly}}. * {{dlc|a}} As none of the Afterbirth transformations unlock an achievement or item, obtaining them is not required for 100% completion of the game in terms of achievements. However, this is only true if you unlocked {{i|Mom's Contacts}} on Rebirth, as the [[Yes Mother?]] transformation will happen once you collect three Mom items on Afterbirth and Afterbirth †. * {{dlc|nr}} Many of the Afterbirth transformations override the charge animations of items such as {{i|Monstro's Lung}} and {{i|Brimstone}}, potentially making them more difficult to use. ** {{dlc|a+}} There is now an option to show a charge bar that floats above Isaac. == Gallery == File:Transformations Afterbirth.jpg|Concept art drawn for transformations that would be implemented into the Afterbirth DLC. == Trivia == * In the original ''The Binding of Isaac'' flash game, the transformation into [[Guppy]] was the only one available, making it chronologically the first. ** Rebirth introduced [[Beelzebub]] as the chronologically second transformation. ** Made after Rebirth, the ''Eternal Edition'' of the original ''The Binding of Isaac'' game added another transformation that turned Isaac into an angel after gaining 7 heart containers from [[eternal heart]]s. * The original name for the [[Beelzebub]] transformation was Lord of the Flies. * The reason no new transformations have been added to Repentance is because Edmund and the dev team do not like them, as they view them as significant advantages gained only from being lucky enough to find specific items. This is why Guppy, the most powerful transformation, requires much more effort to get compared to a transformation like Spider Baby. If any new transformations were to be added, they would be purely visual.https://youtu.be/N-tWNv5EJkU?t=2623 == References == {{Reflist}} {{nav}} [[Category:Transformations| ]]
list_page_content.Attributes:
{{header attributes}}

'''Attributes''', also known as '''stats''', are the numerical values of a [[character]] that affect their performance and the behavior of their [[tears]], Isaac's primary weapon. All characters start with a different set of values that can be increased or decreased during a run. The seven main stats are:
* {{attribute|Health}}: The amount of damage Isaac can take before dying.
* {{attribute|Speed}}: How quickly Isaac moves.
* {{attribute|Tears}}: Isaac's rate of fire. The lower the number, the faster tears are fired.
** {{Dlc|r}} As of ''Repentance'', Isaac's tear rate is shown in tears fired per second; the higher the number, the faster tears are fired.
* {{attribute|Damage}}: The amount of damage that each of Isaac's tears deal to enemies.
* {{attribute|Range}}: How far Isaac's tears travel before landing.
* {{attribute|Shot speed}}: The speed at which Isaac's tears travel.
* {{attribute|Luck}}: Affects a variety of chance-based effects.

Additionally, there are a few [[Hidden Attributes]].

== Stat Screen ==
Most attributes can be viewed on the pause menu, except for health (which is shown in the game [[HUD]]). The pause menu's attributes are shown on an imprecise scale from 1 to 7 ({{dlc|r}} 0 to 7), and the values are as follows:

{{dlc|a}} The pause menu stat screen is made almost completely irrelevant by the introduction of the "Found Hud" feature. Once it is enabled from the in-game options, Isaac's stats will be displayed in numerical form below the key count.

== Found HUD ==
Some stats on the Found HUD are calculated and displayed differently from the internal, technical side of stats.
* '''Damage''': Simply displays base damage per tear, without considering any special tear effects.
* '''Tears''': Displayed as {{dlc|nr}} "tear delay" or {{dlc|r}} "tears per second".
** {{dlc|nr}} To convert the internal tear delay to "Tears per second", use this formula: <math>\frac{30}{\text{Tear Delay} + 1}</math>.
* '''Range''': Displayed as approximate {{dlc|nr}} "tear lifespan" per tear or {{dlc|r}} "range in tiles".
** {{dlc|nr}} There is no straightforward conversion from tear lifespan to range in tiles, as tear falling speed and shot speed are also involved.

list_page_content.Bosses:
{{header bosses}}

'''Bosses''' are very strong [[monster]]s found in [[Boss Room]]s at the end of a floor. The boss at the end of the floor must be defeated to advance to the next floor. From [[Chapter 2]] onwards, many of these non-final bosses can spawn as degraded bosses, or normal [[enemy|enemies]]. The same can go for mini-bosses, which can spawn on later floors as degraded mini-bosses. However, some bosses and mini-bosses cannot appear as degraded bosses at all. The boss for a particular floor is chosen from the pool of bosses for the [[chapter]]s of the floor. After defeating {{e|Satan}} for the first time, bosses may also spawn as [[champion]]s, which can change various attributes such as health, damage, and attack pattern. Upon defeat, most bosses drop an item from the [[Boss (Item Pool)|Boss item pool]] as well as 1-2 [[Heart]]s of any kind.

Bosses boast additional resistances to [[status effect]]s. Once a boss has been inflicted with a status, they become immune to further ones for a period of time lasting longer than the initial effect itself. Slowdown-causing items that require the enemy to stay within their area such as {{i|Night Light}} are nearly useless on bosses due to the immunity triggering from the first tick. On the other hand, whole-room slowdown and slowing creep, such as that from {{i|Stop Watch}} and {{i|Juicy Sack}}, ignore the immunity and affect bosses like normal enemies.

Additionally, some floors may spawn a room with a [[mini-boss]].

In total, there are 102 bosses and 20 mini-bosses. 10 bosses were added in ''[[The Binding of Isaac: Afterbirth]]'', 6 were added in ''[[The Binding of Isaac: Afterbirth †]]'', and 30 bosses and 2 mini-bosses were added in ''[[The Binding of Isaac: Repentance]]''.

== Trivia ==
* Originally, every boss and enemy in Binding of Isaac was going to resemble Isaac in some way.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20170923040329/http://edmundm.com/post/51416553970/binding-of-isaac-fast-facts-now-they-need-to-do</ref> This is still the case for some.
* Double Trouble boss pools can have bosses from previous chapters {{dlc|nr}} and can be the same for both versions of that chapter's floor. This means that, for example, Double Trouble boss fights in The Caves can feature bosses from The Cellar or The Basement, {{dlc|nr}} and every Double Trouble fight in The Caves can also be encountered in The Catacombs.
* Bosses often come in three versions: normal (for example, {{e|Monstro}}), posthumous ({{e|Monstro II}}), and alternate ({{e|Gish}}). Usually, the posthumous boss looks like the original with some horrible mutilation; for example, Monstro and {{e|Chub}} had most of their skin ripped off.
** Most bosses and their alternate/posthumous forms either share similar death animations or share the fact that they lack death animations.
* Some bosses appear as evolved or larger versions of enemies (for example, Chub looks like a bigger {{e|Charger}}). Some even act as larger versions of other bosses ({{e|Little Horn}} and {{e|Big Horn}}).

list_page_content.Monsters:
'''Monsters''' refers to the many antagonistic creatures and entities Isaac can encounter throughout the game. Most monsters (with few exceptions) damage Isaac by either touching him or shooting him with projectiles. Less frequently, monsters spawn additional, lesser monsters, spread [[creep]], or attempt to affect Isaac's movement. Some monsters have [[Stage HP|scaled health]] depending on which floor they're encountered. Along with the more common variety of monsters, [[Champions|champion]] versions can be found lurking about; these monsters are often like their common counterparts, but have a color tint or size change, more health, and drop basic [[pickup]]s on death. There are also champion versions of most [[bosses]]. There are 336 naturally spawning monsters in total. 40 of them were added in [[The Binding of Isaac: Afterbirth|''Afterbirth'']], 21 were added in ''[[Afterbirth †]]'' (13 were added in [[Booster Pack]]s) and 140 were added in [[The Binding of Isaac: Repentance|''Repentance'']]. Almost all of these naturally spawning entities are featured in the {{dlc|a+}} [[Bestiary|Afterbirth+ Bestiary]] or {{dlc|r}} [[Bestiary Repentance|Repentance Bestiary]]. __TOC__ == Monster List == '''Pickups''' are minor items that can be found throughout the game.

'''Pickups can be appear in any of the following locations:'''
{|
 |
* Placed semi-randomly inside rooms.
* Dropped from {{E|Chest}}s.
* Dropped by any kind of {{M|Beggar}}.
 |
* Dropped after clearing a [[room]].
* Found after blasting [[Tinted Rock]]s.
* Dropped by various {{M|Slot Machine}}s.
 |
* Dropped after defeating a [[Champion|Champion monster]].
* Bought in {{R|Shop}}s.
* Generated as part of an [[item]] effect.
 |}

== Notes ==
* Most of the pickups that a player gets on a run will be from clearing rooms. For the logic involved in this, see [[Room Clear Awards]].
* When the "KEEP AWAY" [[seed]] is enabled, all pickups will mimic Isaac's movements.
* {{dlc|a}} When the "GONE SOON" seed is enabled, all pickups will quickly fade.
* When the "CAMO DROP" seed is enabled, all pickups (and [[item]]s) will blend into the ground.

list_page_content.Curses: Every floor Isaac reaches has a chance to be '''cursed''', which hides or alters game elements to make the floor more difficult to complete. Each curse only lasts for the current floor. Possible curses include: {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible" ! style="text-align: center; width: 20%" | Curse ! style="text-align: center; width: 80%" | Description |- | {{curse|blind}} | All [[item]] sprites are replaced with a question mark and are not revealed until they are picked up. |- | {{curse|darkness}} | The floor is much darker, and is only barely lit by Isaac's natural aura. [[Room]]s are occasionally filled with swarms of intangible glowing particles. Fire, explosions, and lasers all cast light as normal, as does [[red creep]]. {{Dlc|r}} The aura of light around Isaac is much larger, but the darkness outside it is much darker. |- | {{curse|lost}} | Removes the map from the [[HUD]], similarly to the [[Amnesia]] pill, and increases the possible total room count of the current floor by 4. |- | {{curse|maze}} | Entering a new room (including teleporting) will occasionally takes Isaac to the wrong room, with a screen-shake and sound effect to indicate the jump. For instance, entering the room to the right may instead take Isaac to the room to the left. Occasionally, discovered rooms with identical door layouts can swap contents, without a screen-shake or sound effect. {{dlc|r}} Curse of the Maze is now supposed to only teleport Isaac into uncleared rooms, but this is bugged (See bugs section) and no longer triggers a screen-shake. |- | {{curse|unknown}} | Removes Isaac's [[health]] from the [[HUD]], leaving the player unable to see how many hearts remain of any kind. All health is still tracked as normal, and Isaac still urinates on the floor by the door when he enters a room at half a heart of total HP. {{Dlc|r}} Also removes the {{I|Holy Mantle}} icon from the HUD. |- | {{curse|labyrinth}} | Appears only on the first floor of a [[chapter]]. Merges the two floors of a chapter; the resulting XL floor contains two {{R|Boss Room}}s, two {{R|Treasure Room}}s, and counts as two floors. |- | {{curse|cursed}} | Changes normal doors into [[Curse Room|cursed doors]]. Found only in the [[Cursed!]] [[challenge]] or when playing with the {{dlc|a}} "CVRS ED" [[seed]]. |- | {{curse|giant}} | Merges normal-sized rooms into 2x2, 1x2, 2x1, or L-shaped rooms while narrow rooms are not affected ''(further confimation needed)''. This curse currently isn't achievable naturally in game. |} == Chance == Odds of getting a curse vary with progression, increasing over time. {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible striped" ! style="width: 20%" | Unlocks ! style="width: 40%" | Normal ! style="width: 40%" | Hard |- | New Game | 1/80 | 1/10 |- | Killed {{E|Mom}} | 1/30 | 1/5 |- | Everything is Terrible | 1/10 | 1/3 |- | Killed {{E|Isaac}} | 1/5 | {{dlc|nr}} 1/2 or {{dlc|r}} 1/3 |} * All curses have an equal, 1/6 chance of occurring. ** If Curse of the Labyrinth is picked on a floor it cannot spawn on, such as the second floor of any chapter, [[Greed mode]], or on any chapters post-Womb, no curses will be active for the floor. *** This makes the chance of getting cursed on floors without Curse of the Labyrinth 1/6, or {{dlc|nr}} 5/12 or {{dlc|r}} 5/18 for [[Hard Mode]] if {{E|Isaac}} has been defeated. == Notes == * {{curse|labyrinth}} can spawn the same [[boss]] in both rooms on one floor. * If {{curse|Labyrinth}} is encountered in {{s|Basement}}, {{s|Cellar}}, or the {{s|Burning Basement}}, both {{R|Treasure Room}} doors will be unlocked. * As Curse of the Labyrinth causes the two floors in a [[chapter]] to merge, the {{R|Devil Room}} or [[Angel Room]] (if playing as [[Bethany]] or the player is holding [[Rosary Bead]]) can be encountered in {{s|Basement}} XL, {{s|Cellar}} XL, or {{s|Burning Basement}} XL, and the chances to do so are lowered as usual if Isaac takes red heart damage. * With the Curse of the Labyrinth, it is possible to teleport into the second {{R|Boss Room}} and completely skip the first. * {{dlc+|r}} If Curse of the Labyrinth takes effect during [[Chapter 1]], taking the door that spawns after the second boss room will lead to {{s|Downpour}} II or {{s|Dross}} II.{{dlc-}} * {{dlc+|r}} If Curse of the Labyrinth merges {{s|Downpour}} or {{S|Dross}}, there will be two "mirror" Boss Rooms.{{dlc-}} * {{dlc+|r}} If Curse of the Labyrinth merges {{s|Downpour}}, {{s|Dross}}, {{s|Mines}}, or {{s|Ashpit}} into an XL floor, the special rooms leading to {{i|Knife Piece 1}} and {{i|Knife Piece 2}} and their associated gimmicks will still appear. ** Only one of the "mirror" treasure rooms in {{s|Downpour}} and {{s|Dross}} will contain the knife piece; the other will be empty.{{dlc-}} * {{r|Red Room}}s can be created adjacent to the first {{r|Boss Room}} if {{cu|Labyrinth}} takes effect on the floor. * {{curse|darkness}} can be made somewhat easier by increasing the gamma setting in the game's [[options]]. ** {{dlc+|r}} {{p|XIX - The Sun}} can now remove Curse of Darkness for the current floor.{{dlc-}} ** {{dlc+|a}} The {{i|Night Light}} can remove Curse of Darkness for the floor it's picked up. While it does not make Isaac immune to the Curse in floors after picking it up, the light beam it emits diminishes the drawback of low visibility.{{dlc-}} ** Interactions and attacks that have a controlled crosshair have their own little highlight. Controlled characters such as {{i|Strawman}} / {{p|Soul of the Forgotten}} / {{p|Soul of Jacob and Esau}} / {{t|Found Soul}} have their own full lighting like Isaac. * {{curse|unknown}} does not affect {{C|The Lost}}, as he lacks health to begin with. ** {{Dlc+|r}} The Lost can no longer see whether the {{I|Holy Mantle}}'s shield has been broken in the current room.{{dlc-}} * {{curse|maze}} can be exploited to enter all types of locked rooms without spending resources. Be warned, though, as trying to enter a {{R|Curse Room}} can teleport Isaac elsewhere, dealing damage without getting him into the desired room (unless he has some form of [[flight]]). * When Curse of the Maze triggers, it takes into account the direction the room was exited through. For example, if Isaac leaves a room by a door on the right wall, it can only take Isaac to a room that has a door on its left wall. (This also works with teleporting.) * Since Curse of the Maze can only swap rooms with identical door layouts, if one of the rooms has a wall where the other had a door, a secret room is guaranteed to be there. * Curse of the Maze can interfere with teleportation cards, but {{p|Joker}} is unaffected. * With Curse of the Maze active, using any form of extra life or revival item can put Isaac into a previously unvisited room. * {{dlc+|nr}} Curse of the Maze can teleport Isaac to the same room he was just in.{{dlc-}} * Items that reveal the entire map do not counteract the effect of Curse of the Lost. * With {{curse|blind}} active, leaving and re-entering a room which had a revealed item will change the item back to a question mark. * {{dlc|r}} If an item is affected by {{achievement|Corrupted Data|n}}, it is revealed upon first entering the room even if {{curse|blind}} is active. Leaving the room and returning will render it hidden again. * {{plat|3DS}} Curse of the Lost can be countered, as the player can draw a map on the touch screen. * When activating curses via the console, curses are calculated using powers of 2 and addition. To apply multiple curses, add the number of each desired curse together. For example, curse 43 would give Isaac Curse of Darkness, Curse of Labyrinth, Curse of the Unknown, and Curse of the Maze (1+2+8+32 = 43). ** Curse of Darkness = 1. ** Curse of Labyrinth = 2. ** Curse of the Lost = 4. ** Curse of the Unknown = 8. ** Curse of the Cursed = 16. ** Curse of the Maze = 32. ** Curse of the Blind = 64. ** {{dlc|r}} Curse of the Giant = 128. == Gallery == File:questionmark.png|The question mark icon that hides [[items]] when Curse of the Blind is active == Trivia == * Of the three curses that returned from ''Wrath of the Lamb,'' (Labyrinth, the Lost, and Darkness), two of them — {{curse|lost}} and {{curse|darkness}} — have different effects from ''Rebirth'' onwards. ** The Curse of Darkness used to hide the map, but was given the floor darkening effect in Rebirth. ** The Curse of the Lost used to simply add 4 rooms to the possible total room count of a floor, and was also given the map removal effect from Curse of Darkness. ** The {{curse|labyrinth}} remained untouched. * {{curse|giant}} originated from the ''Antibirth'' mod, but cannot be encountered in-game without using mods or the [[Debug Console]]. This was because the room-combining aspect of the curse would often lead to buggy or unfair rooms. ** A system was to be added that determined the fairness of a large room, such as limiting the number of enemies, but this proved to be too complicated to finish in time. * {{dlc+|r}} There is another curse being used internally in the game, called '''Curse of the Mist'''. This is active inside the {{E|Mother's Shadow}} area and is used to disable Isaac's items.{{dlc-}} == Bugs == {{Bug|dlc=r|{{curse|maze}} can still teleport Isaac to already explored rooms.}} {{nav|fr=Fléaux}} [[Category:Mechanics]]

'''Seeds''', essentially random strings of characters, are used as the basis for all decision making in the game. By inputting a seed manually, one can guarantee that most randomly-generated decisions will occur the same every time. For instance, two players using the same seed will find the same items in the same locations. == Notes Regarding Seed Entry == * Seeds are the same on every platform as long as the game version is also the same. For instance, if you find a Rebirth seed on PC, you can use it on the 3DS version and it will be the same because the 3DS version only has Rebirth. * Except for some special seeds, inputting a seed will cause the 'no trophies' mark to appear. This prevents any achievements from being obtained on that particular run. * All alphanumeric characters are accepted except for 5, I, O, and U. On the PC version pressing the I, O, and U keys during seed entry replace them with 1, 0, and V respectively. * {{dlc|a}} Once a special seed has been entered, it will be permanently unlocked without needing to enter the code again. Pressing '''Q''' / '''Left Bumper''' during character selection will bring up the Easter Egg Section, in which any previously unlocked game mode can be selected. Selecting multiple modes at once is also possible. * On the special seed selection menu, it is possible to remove a discovered seed by focusing it and pressing Q+E+Tab on keyboard or LB+RB+X on controller. * In total, there are 69 special seeds. == Special Seeds == {| class="mw-collapsible wikitable striped" style="text-align:center;width:100%;height:100%;" !style="width:30px"|'''ID''' !style="width:100px"|'''Seed''' !style="width:300px"|'''Description''' !'''Effects''' |- !colspan="5" | Miscellaneous |- |16 |BASE

Instead, the Soul hearts and/or Black hearts will be completely depleted, leaving Isaac with only his Red hearts. You can also prevent death with {{e|Bone Heart}}s, as they will get depleted without destroying the bone heart and protecting the rest of the hearts, and can be refilled with Red Hearts. The soul hearts in front of them will get destroyed though. * If the ICES KATE seed is enabled, purple champion monsters are be much more dangerous, as escaping their gravitational pull becomes almost impossible. * When playing with the KAPP A seed enabled, certain items will override the grayscale, such as {{i|Kidney Stone}}. * When the seed PACI FISM is enabled, effects that kill enemies on impact such as {{I|Euthanasia}} and {{I|Little Horn}} still remove enemies, but they will respawn when the room is re-entered. No effect occurs after killing all in a room. * Some seeds can not be enabled alongside other certain seeds. For example, the PTCH BLCK seed cannot be enabled alongside THEB LANK. * It is possible to access other chapters while playing the BASE MENT seed. Stepping into the beam of light after using {{i|Genesis}} can take Isaac to chapter II, avoiding the standard loop after the first two basement floors. Additionally, {{R|Sacrifice Room}}s after 12 uses can be used at any point to teleport to the {{s|Dark Room}}, making it possible to see a proper ending and finish the run. == Trivia == * Many of the special seeds can be found by using either the {{M|Fortune Telling Machine}} or the {{P|Rules Card}}. * Some special seeds were shared with the community by the developers. * If an [[item]] that would appear on a seed is not unlocked yet, it will be replaced by another item that is unlocked. * The "DYSL EXIA" seed is a reference to [[Wikipedia:Dyslexia|dyslexia]], a disorder that causes difficulties in spelling, reading, and writing. * The seeds "ANDA NTE", "LARG HET0", "ALLE GR0", and "PRES T0" refer to ''andante'', ''larghetto'', ''allegro'', and ''presto'', respectively. These are names of musical [[wikipedia:Tempo|tempos]], which are the speeds of given pieces of music. * The "KAPP A" seed is a reference to a commonly used Twitch emote "KAPPA". * The "ALM1 GHTY" seed's description is, "Dog Mode". This is referencing the term "God mode", which is used to describe someone or something in a game that has invincibility, usually attained through the use of cheats. ** "Dog Mode" is also a reference to the 1994 first-person shooter game ''[[wikipedia:Rise of the Triad|Rise of the Triad]]'', which has a power-up of the same name. * The "FEAR M1NT" seed is a reference to [[wikipedia:spearmint|spearmint]], an herb popularly used for flavoring food. * Although the "T1NY D0ME" seed also makes Isaac's hitbox somewhat smaller and could be considered beneficial, it does not prevent achievements. * {{dlc|r}} The "SVPE RH0T" seed is a [[References|reference]] to the 2016 first-person shooter game ''[[wikipedia:Superhot|Superhot]]''. * {{dlc|r}} "M0DE SEVN" i.e. Mode 7 is a [[wikipedia:Mode 7|graphics mode]] on the Super Nintendo Entertainment System console that often results in pronounced pixelation. * {{dlc|r}}"GFVE LLLL" is used as 11/4/2022 and 3/24/2023's daily runs. * {{dlc|r}} While using {{c|Tainted Forgotten}} in the "SVPE RH0T" seed, time will only move when the skeleton moves. == Bugs == {{bug|dlc=a|Entering the [[Blue Womb]] while using the "N0RE TVRN" seed is not recommended as the door leading to the Boss Room will not spawn, thereby trapping the player and ending the run.}} {{bug|Returning to the menu from a run that uses a seed that alters audio speed will cause the main menu audio to remain altered. This resets upon entering a new run, or exiting the game.}} {{bug|dlc=r|The PAC1F1SM seed does not allow you to finish a The Beast run. Dogma never transforms in his second phase. If you manage to go to the Beast fight (with the debug console), the Chest spawns before Ultra Famine is killed.}} {{nav|fr=Graines}} [[Category:Mechanics]]
